title: trifolium angustifolium subsp. purpureum
intro: Trifolium angustifolium subsp. purpureum, also known as Purple Clover, is a subspecies of the Trifolium angustifolium plant, belonging to the legume family and native to North America. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties, derived from its isoflavone content. The health benefits of Purple Clover include its potential to alleviate symptoms of menopause, improve cardiovascular health, and support bone health. Purple Clover is typically consumed through herbal preparations such as tea, tincture, and capsule, with recommended dosages varying depending on the specific preparation and intended use.
